summary: Crystal‑clear Charlie Miller soundboard capture of a tight Greek run night, highlighted by a rippin Deal solo, flawless Lazy/Supplication jam and tight China Cat Sunflower/Riders on the Storm transitions.
review: The show is praised as one of the best nights of the May ’82 Greek run – the band was tight, with standout performances of Deal, Lazy Lightnin'/Supplication, Tennessee Jed and the China Cat Sunflower/Riders sequence. The Charlie Miller soundboard recording is described as fantastic, clear and balanced, making the musical excellence shine through without any noticeable flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: All detailed reviews are overwhelmingly positive about both performance and sound quality; recording is praised as excellent and the musical show is described as tight with multiple standout moments.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Charlie Miller mix is crystal‑clear, well balanced and free of dropouts; fans repeatedly note the fantastic sound throughout.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower/Riders on the Storm
show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert flows tightly with strong first and second sets; jams are concise yet energetic, keeping momentum high throughout the Greek run.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Deal solo called 'most rippin' ever' and Tennessee Jed solo described as a clever, story‑like guitar narrative.
